name: Surva — International Festival of the Masquerade Games
dates: Last two weekends of January (2026: January 16–25; parades on January 17–18 and 24–25)
description: The largest masquerade festival on the Balkan Peninsula, held in the town of Pernik, Bulgaria. In ancient Bulgaria, wearing masks was believed to provide protection from evil, ensure a successful harvest, and guarantee health and fertility. Many of these pagan rituals (incorporated into Christianity) are still performed in Bulgaria between Christmas and Easter. Rituals held around the New Year are known as Kukeri; those performed around Easter are known as Surva. At the Surva festival, single men and women called Kukeri and Survakari don traditional Bulgarian costumes and elaborate masks and parade through Pernik's streets. Around 6,000 people (~100 groups) from every folklore region of Bulgaria plus teams from Europe, Asia, and Africa participate. Pernik was accepted into the International Federation of Carnival Cities in 1995. In June 2009, the festival was proclaimed the European Capital of Survas and Mummers and included in UNESCO's Intangible Heritage of Humanity list.
